steps.title: Expand to standard form
steps.content:
Expand the left side: $x(x+9) - 15 = 0$ becomes $x^2 + 9x - 15 = 0$.
From the standard form $ax^2 + bx + c = 0$, identify the coefficients: $a = 1$, $b = 9$, $c = -15$.

steps.title: Apply the quadratic formula
steps.content:
Calculate the discriminant: $b^2 - 4ac = 9^2 - 4(1)(-15) = 81 + 60 = 141$.
Substitute into the formula: $$x = \frac{-9 \pm \sqrt{141}}{2}$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
